calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-19-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth Salverson, Deputy City Attorney, and Philip Ginsberg, Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3809, for Petitioners and Moving Party City and County of San Francisco
Vincent A. Harrington, Jr., Esq., Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (510) 839-6600 for Respondents and Opposing Party Services Employees International Union Locals 250, 535 and 790
RULING:
A R G U E D; The Court concludes that the parties negotiated a valid contract, which has within it impasse resolution procedures. The interpretation of that contract is a matter of law for the court to decide. The Court finds that, while there may be a good faith disagreement between the parties, there is no claim by either side that the other is failing to negotiate or otherwise acting in bad faith. Accordingly, the Court rejects Respondent's argument that this is a matter that must be referred in the first instance to the California Public Employment Resolution Board (PERB). Moreover, even if reference to PERB were appropriate, to do so at this stage of the negotiations would result in irreparable harm to Petitioner.

Petitioner City and County of San Francisco's Petition to Compel Arbitration pursuant to CCP 1281.2 is GRANTED. The parties are ordered to conduct the impasse resolution procedures pursuant to 8.409-4 of the San Francisco City Charter. Order signed. (301/JLW/DS) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
